meta.book: Daniel (Addition: Prayer of Azariah and Song of the Three Young Men)

preamble.summary: Inserted between Daniel 3:23 and 3:24, this addition contains two compositions: the Prayer of Azariah (vv. 1-22), a national confession and petition uttered from inside the fiery furnace, and the Song of the Three Young Men (vv. 43-68), a cosmic hymn calling all creation to praise God. A brief narrative section (vv. 35-42) describes the angel of the Lord descending to protect the three.
preamble.remarkable: The Song of the Three Young Men (the Benedicite) became one of the most widely used liturgical texts in Christian history. It appears in the Lauds of the Roman Office, the Anglican Book of Common Prayer, and Orthodox morning services. The hymn's cosmic scope — calling heavens, waters, fire, frost, mountains, and all living things to praise — anticipates Psalm 148 and Francis of Assisi's Canticle of the Sun.
preamble.friction: These 68 verses have no counterpart in the Hebrew/Aramaic MT. They interrupt the narrative between 3:23 (the three are thrown in) and 3:24 (Nebuchadnezzar is astonished). The prayer's theology of exile and the absence of temple sacrifice reflects a post-587 BCE Jewish perspective, possibly composed in the Maccabean period.
preamble.connections: Daniel 3:23-24 (narrative context); Psalm 136 (litany structure); Psalm 148 (all creation praising); Exodus 15 (Song of the Sea); Genesis 1 (creation order mirrored in the Benedicite).

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): Καὶ περιεπάτουν ἐν μέσῳ τῆς φλογός
verses.text_reference: They walked about in the midst of the flames, singing hymns to God and blessing the Lord.
verses.rendering: And they walked about in the midst of the flames, singing hymns to God and blessing the Lord.
translator_notes: This verse bridges MT Daniel 3:23 and 3:24. The three young men — Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah (Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego) — are already in the furnace.

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): καὶ στὰς Αζαριας προσηύξατο οὕτως
verses.text_reference: Then Azariah stood still in the fire and prayed aloud:
verses.rendering: Then Azariah stood in the fire and opened his mouth and prayed thus:
translator_notes: Azariah is the Hebrew name of Abednego. His prayer is a national confession, not merely a personal plea for rescue.

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): Εὐλογητὸς εἶ κύριε ὁ θεὸς τῶν πατέρων ἡμῶν
verses.text_reference: 'Blessed are you, O Lord, God of our ancestors, and worthy of praise.'
verses.rendering: 'Blessed are you, O Lord, God of our fathers, and worthy of praise.

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): καὶ αἰνετὸν τὸ ὄνομά σου εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας
verses.text_reference: 'and glorious is your name forever!'
verses.rendering: And your name is glorious forever!'

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): ὅτι δίκαιος εἶ ἐπὶ πᾶσιν οἷς ἐποίησας ἡμῖν
verses.text_reference: 'For you are just in all you have done; all your works are true and your ways right.'
verses.rendering: 'For you are righteous in all you have done to us; all your works are true and your ways are upright.

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): καὶ πάντα τὰ κρίματά σου ἀληθινά
verses.text_reference: 'and all your judgments are true.'
verses.rendering: And all your judgments are true.

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): ὅτι κρίματα ἀληθείας ἐποίησας κατὰ πάντα
verses.text_reference: 'You have executed true judgments in all the things you have brought upon us and upon Jerusalem, the holy city of our ancestors.'
verses.rendering: You have rendered true judgments in all the things you have brought upon us and upon Jerusalem, the holy city of our fathers.

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): ὅτι ἐν ἀληθείᾳ καὶ κρίσει ἐπήγαγες ταῦτα πάντα
verses.text_reference: 'for in truth and justice you have brought all this upon us because of our sins.'
verses.rendering: For in truth and justice you have brought all this upon us because of our sins.
translator_notes: The prayer acknowledges that the exile is divine judgment — not arbitrary suffering but the covenant consequences of Deuteronomy 28.

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): ὅτι ἡμάρτομεν καὶ ἠνομήσαμεν ἀποστῆναι ἀπὸ σοῦ
verses.text_reference: 'For we have sinned and broken your law in turning away from you.'
verses.rendering: For we have sinned and acted lawlessly in departing from you.

verses.text_reference: 'in all things we have sinned grievously.'
verses.rendering: In all things we have sinned grievously.

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): καὶ τὰς ἐντολάς σου οὐκ ἠκούσαμεν
verses.text_reference: 'We have not obeyed your commandments.'
verses.rendering: We have not listened to your commandments,

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): οὐδὲ συνετηρήσαμεν οὐδὲ ἐποιήσαμεν καθὼς ἐνετείλω ἡμῖν
verses.text_reference: 'we have not kept them or done what you commanded us for our own good.'
verses.rendering: nor have we kept them, nor have we done as you commanded us for our own good.'

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): καὶ πάντα ὅσα ἐπήγαγες ἡμῖν ἐν κρίματι ἀληθινῷ ἐποίησας
verses.text_reference: 'So all that you have brought upon us, and all that you have done to us, you have done in true judgment.'
verses.rendering: 'All that you have brought upon us, and all that you have done to us, you have done in true judgment.

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): καὶ παρέδωκας ἡμᾶς εἰς χεῖρας ἐχθρῶν ἀνόμων
verses.text_reference: 'You have handed us over to our enemies, lawless and hateful rebels.'
verses.rendering: You have handed us over to lawless enemies, hateful rebels,

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): καὶ βασιλεῖ ἀδίκῳ καὶ πονηροτάτῳ παρὰ πᾶσαν τὴν γῆν
verses.text_reference: 'and to an unjust king, the most wicked in all the world.'
verses.rendering: and to an unjust king, the most wicked in all the earth.'
translator_notes: The 'unjust king' is Nebuchadnezzar, though the description also fits Antiochus IV in the historical context of the addition's composition.

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): καὶ νῦν οὐκ ἔστιν ἡμῖν ἀνοῖξαι τὸ στόμα
verses.text_reference: 'And now we cannot open our mouths.'
verses.rendering: 'And now we cannot open our mouths.

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): αἰσχύνη καὶ ὄνειδος ἐγενήθη τοῖς δούλοις σου
verses.text_reference: 'we, your servants who worship you, have become a shame and a reproach.'
verses.rendering: Shame and disgrace have come upon your servants and upon those who worship you.

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): μὴ δὴ παραδῷς ἡμᾶς εἰς τέλος
verses.text_reference: 'For your name's sake do not give us up forever.'
verses.rendering: For the sake of your name, do not hand us over utterly,

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): καὶ μὴ διασκεδάσῃς τὴν διαθήκην σου
verses.text_reference: 'and do not annul your covenant.'
verses.rendering: and do not break your covenant.
translator_notes: The appeal to the covenant — 'do not annul your diathēkē' — grounds the prayer in the Abrahamic and Mosaic promises. Even in judgment, God's covenant fidelity is invoked.

translator_notes.note: Greek diathēkē — the standard LXX rendering of Hebrew berit. The prayer appeals to God's covenant faithfulness as the basis for mercy.

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): καὶ μὴ ἀποστήσῃς τὸ ἔλεός σου ἀφ᾽ ἡμῶν
verses.text_reference: 'Do not withdraw your mercy from us.'
verses.rendering: Do not withdraw your mercy from us,

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): διὰ Αβρααμ τὸν ἠγαπημένον ὑπὸ σοῦ
verses.text_reference: 'for the sake of Abraham your beloved and for the sake of your servant Isaac and Israel your holy one.'
verses.rendering: for the sake of Abraham, your beloved, and Isaac, your servant, and Israel, your holy one,
translator_notes: The triple patriarchal invocation (Abraham, Isaac, Israel) echoes the covenant formula of Exodus 32:13, where Moses intercedes after the golden calf.

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): οἷς ἐλάλησας πληθῦναι τὸ σπέρμα αὐτῶν
verses.text_reference: 'to whom you promised to multiply their descendants like the stars of heaven and like the sand on the shore of the sea.'
verses.rendering: to whom you spoke, promising to multiply their offspring like the stars of heaven and like the sand on the shore of the sea.'

verses.text_reference: 'For we, O Lord, have become fewer than any other nation.'
verses.rendering: 'For we, O Lord, have become fewer than any other nation,

verses.text_reference: 'and are brought low this day in all the world because of our sins.'
verses.rendering: and we are humbled this day throughout all the earth because of our sins.'

verses.text_reference: 'In our day we have no ruler, no prophet, no leader.'
verses.rendering: 'In our day there is no ruler, no prophet, no leader,
translator_notes: The list of what is absent — ruler, prophet, leader, sacrifice, temple — summarizes the total loss of the exile. All institutional mediation between God and Israel is gone. Only prayer remains.

verses.text_reference: 'no burnt offering, no sacrifice, no oblation, no incense, no place to make an offering before you and to find mercy.'
verses.rendering: no burnt offering, no sacrifice, no oblation, no incense, no place to present an offering before you or to find mercy.'

verses.text_reference: 'Yet with a contrite heart and a humble spirit may we be accepted.'
verses.rendering: 'Yet with a contrite heart and a humble spirit, may we be accepted,

verses.text_reference: 'as though it were with burnt offerings of rams and bulls, or with tens of thousands of fat lambs.'
verses.rendering: as though we came with burnt offerings of rams and bulls and with tens of thousands of fat lambs.'

verses.text_reference: 'so may our sacrifice be in your sight today, and may it please you; for no one who puts trust in you will be put to shame.'
verses.rendering: 'So may our sacrifice be in your sight today, and may it be pleasing to you; for those who put their trust in you will never be put to shame.'

verses.text_reference: 'And now with all our heart we follow you; we fear you and seek your presence.'
verses.rendering: 'Now we follow you with all our heart, and we fear you and seek your face.

verses.text_reference: 'Do not put us to shame, but deal with us in your patience and in your abundant mercy.'
verses.rendering: Do not put us to shame, but deal with us according to your patience and your great mercy.

verses.text_reference: 'Deliver us in accordance with your marvelous works, and bring glory to your name, O Lord.'
verses.rendering: Deliver us according to your wondrous works, and give glory to your name, O Lord.'

verses.text_reference: 'Let all who do harm to your servants be put to shame.'
verses.rendering: 'Let all who harm your servants be put to shame.

verses.text_reference: 'Let them be disgraced and deprived of all power, and let their strength be broken.'
verses.rendering: Let them be disgraced and stripped of all their power, and let their strength be shattered.'

verses.text_reference: Now the king's servants who threw them in did not stop making the furnace hot.
verses.rendering: Now the king's servants who had thrown them in did not cease stoking the furnace

verses.text_reference: with naphtha, pitch, tow, and brushwood.
verses.rendering: with naphtha, pitch, tow, and brushwood.
translator_notes: Naphtha — a petroleum product — appears in Greek sources as a Babylonian commodity. Its inclusion reflects authentic local knowledge.

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): καὶ ἐξεχεῖτο ἡ φλὸξ ἐπάνω τῆς καμίνου
verses.text_reference: And the flames streamed forth above the furnace forty-nine cubits.
verses.rendering: And the flame poured out above the furnace forty-nine cubits.

verses.text_reference: and it spread and burned those Chaldeans who were caught near the furnace.
verses.rendering: And it spread out and burned those Chaldeans whom it caught near the furnace.

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): ὁ δὲ ἄγγελος κυρίου κατέβη
verses.text_reference: But the angel of the Lord came down into the furnace to be with Azariah and his companions.
verses.rendering: But the angel of the Lord came down into the furnace to be with Azariah and his companions,
translator_notes: The angel of the Lord — the figure Nebuchadnezzar sees in MT Daniel 3:25 — is here explicitly identified. The narrative bridge explains how the three survived.

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): καὶ ἐξετίναξεν τὴν φλόγα τοῦ πυρός
verses.text_reference: and drove the fiery flame out of the furnace.
verses.rendering: and drove the fiery flame out of the furnace.

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): καὶ ἐποίησεν τὸ μέσον τῆς καμίνου ὡς πνεῦμα δρόσου διασυρίζον
verses.text_reference: and made the inside of the furnace as though a moist wind were whistling through it.
verses.rendering: And he made the inside of the furnace like a moist wind whistling through,

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): καὶ οὐχ ἥψατο αὐτῶν τὸ καθόλου τὸ πῦρ
verses.text_reference: The fire did not touch them at all and caused them no pain or distress.
verses.rendering: and the fire did not touch them at all, and caused them neither pain nor distress.

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): Τότε οἱ τρεῖς ὡς ἐξ ἑνὸς στόματος ὕμνουν
verses.text_reference: Then the three with one voice praised and glorified and blessed God in the furnace:
verses.rendering: Then the three, as from one mouth, praised and glorified and blessed God in the furnace, saying:
translator_notes: The Song of the Three Young Men (the Benedicite) begins here. It became one of the most important canticles in Christian liturgy, used in morning prayer in the Anglican, Roman, and Orthodox traditions.

verses.text_source: Greek (Th): Εὐλογητὸς εἶ κύριε ὁ θεὸς τῶν πατέρων ἡμῶν
verses.text_reference: 'Blessed are you, O Lord, God of our ancestors; worthy of praise and glorified forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Blessed are you, O Lord, God of our fathers — worthy of praise and glorified forever.'

verses.text_reference: 'Blessed is your glorious, holy name; to be praised and glorified above all forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Blessed is your glorious, holy name — to be highly praised and highly glorified forever.'

verses.text_reference: 'Blessed are you in the temple of your holy glory; to be extolled and glorified above all forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Blessed are you in the temple of your holy glory — to be praised and glorified above all forever.'

verses.text_reference: 'Blessed are you who look into the depths from your throne on the cherubim; to be praised and glorified above all forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Blessed are you who look into the depths from your throne upon the cherubim — to be praised and glorified above all forever.'

verses.text_reference: 'Blessed are you on the throne of your kingdom; to be extolled and glorified above all forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Blessed are you on the throne of your kingdom — to be praised and glorified above all forever.'

verses.text_reference: 'Blessed are you in the firmament of heaven; to be sung and glorified forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Blessed are you in the firmament of heaven — to be praised and glorified forever.'

verses.text_reference: 'Bless the Lord, all you works of the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Bless the Lord, all you works of the Lord — praise and exalt him above all forever.'
translator_notes: This refrain — 'Bless the Lord... praise and exalt him above all forever' — structures the entire canticle. It systematically calls all creation to worship, moving from the cosmic (heavens, angels) through the atmospheric (rain, wind, fire) to the terrestrial (earth, mountains). The Benedicite mirrors the creation order of Genesis 1.

verses.text_reference: 'Bless the Lord, you heavens; praise and exalt him above all forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Bless the Lord, you heavens — praise and exalt him above all forever.'

verses.text_reference: 'Bless the Lord, you angels of the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Bless the Lord, you angels of the Lord — praise and exalt him above all forever.'

verses.text_reference: 'Bless the Lord, all you waters above the heavens; praise and exalt him above all forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Bless the Lord, all you waters above the heavens — praise and exalt him above all forever.'

verses.text_reference: 'Bless the Lord, all you powers of the Lord; praise and exalt him above all forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Bless the Lord, all you powers of the Lord — praise and exalt him above all forever.'

verses.text_reference: 'Bless the Lord, sun and moon; praise and exalt him above all forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Bless the Lord, sun and moon — praise and exalt him above all forever.'

verses.text_reference: 'Bless the Lord, stars of heaven; praise and exalt him above all forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Bless the Lord, stars of heaven — praise and exalt him above all forever.'

verses.text_reference: 'Bless the Lord, all rain and dew; praise and exalt him above all forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Bless the Lord, all rain and dew — praise and exalt him above all forever.'

verses.text_reference: 'Bless the Lord, all you winds; praise and exalt him above all forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Bless the Lord, all you winds — praise and exalt him above all forever.'

verses.text_reference: 'Bless the Lord, fire and heat; praise and exalt him above all forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Bless the Lord, fire and heat — praise and exalt him above all forever.'
translator_notes: The irony of calling 'fire and heat' to bless the Lord while standing unharmed in a furnace is the narrative heart of the canticle.

verses.text_reference: 'Bless the Lord, winter cold and summer heat; praise and exalt him above all forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Bless the Lord, winter cold and summer heat — praise and exalt him above all forever.'

verses.text_reference: 'Bless the Lord, dews and falling snow; praise and exalt him above all forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Bless the Lord, dews and falling snow — praise and exalt him above all forever.'

verses.text_reference: 'Bless the Lord, ice and cold; praise and exalt him above all forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Bless the Lord, ice and cold — praise and exalt him above all forever.'

verses.text_reference: 'Bless the Lord, frosts and snows; praise and exalt him above all forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Bless the Lord, frosts and snows — praise and exalt him above all forever.'

verses.text_reference: 'Bless the Lord, nights and days; praise and exalt him above all forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Bless the Lord, nights and days — praise and exalt him above all forever.'

verses.text_reference: 'Bless the Lord, light and darkness; praise and exalt him above all forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Bless the Lord, light and darkness — praise and exalt him above all forever.'

verses.text_reference: 'Bless the Lord, lightnings and clouds; praise and exalt him above all forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Bless the Lord, lightnings and clouds — praise and exalt him above all forever.'

verses.text_reference: 'Let the earth bless the Lord; let it praise and exalt him above all forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Let the earth bless the Lord — let it praise and exalt him above all forever.'

verses.text_reference: 'Bless the Lord, mountains and hills; praise and exalt him above all forever.'
verses.rendering: 'Bless the Lord, mountains and hills — praise and exalt him above all forever.'
